_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
condition: Penectomy, Penile prosthesis insertion (For Benign Condition)
source_files: Penectomy, Penile prosthesis insertion (For Benign Condition)_Medco.pdf
Penectomy, Penile prosthesis insertion (For Benign Condition)_PPD.CPD.pdf
clinical_indications: Fournier gangrene which includes complications due to Diabetes.

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management, advise for the procedure.

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management, advise for the procedure submitted?
Patient presented with proliferative lesion involving penis, recurrent infection, or bleeding?

cpd_claim.q: Detailed Indoor case papers with daily vitals and line of treatment available?
Receipt for specimen submitted for Histopathology for penectomy procedure?
Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge available?

common_queries: Patient not presented with proliferative lesion involving penis, recurrent infection, or bleeding for penectomy
Clinical notes for pre-authorization missing or incomplete
Detailed Indoor case papers with daily vitals and line of treatment missing at claim submission
Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes missing at claim submission
Receipt for specimen submitted for Histopathology for penectomy procedure missing at claim submission
Discharge summary with follow-up advise missing at claim submission

surgical_options: Penectomy involves removal of whole penis or partial.
In total penectomy removal of penis, urethra and penile root further the surgeon creates a new urinary opening in the perineum.
